title: corytholoma douglasii var. verticillatum
intro: Corytholoma douglasii var. verticillatum, also known as Douglas' corytholoma, is a variety of flowering plant in the corydalis family, native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant's root and rhizome contain alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Corytholoma douglasii var. verticillatum include relief from muscle and joint pain, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
